title: Gas report - Week 31
summary:
LNG
The LNG market traded largely sideways this week, following a relatively quiet period of activity. With tonnage lists remaining stable and limited fresh enquiry, market sentiment remained flat across most routes. On the BLNG1 Australia-Japan route, rates for 174k cbm vessels slipped $1,500 to $33,400 per day, while 160k cbm units held steady at $22,200, reflecting muted Pacific activity and weak short-term demand.

children.title: LNG
children.summary: The LNG market traded largely sideways this week, following a relatively quiet period of activity. With tonnage lists remaining stable and limited fresh enquiry, market sentiment remained flat across most routes.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: The LPG market was active this week, though rates eased slightly as charterers appeared to reach a ceiling. While vessel supply remained broadly balanced, appetite for pushing rates higher diminished.
